items.title: Public hearing into hiring of new CIT CEO cancelled as probe continues

children.content: The chair of the CIT board told an ACT Legislative Assembly inquiry the complaints made against chief executive Margot McNeill related to perceptions of conflicts of interest around employments and contracts.

image.altText: A woman with classes in a green suit.

items.title: Draft report into ANU's controversial cuts critical of university's council

children.content: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

image.altText: Signage for the ANU.

items.title: 'A succulent Chinese meal?': These are the 2026 Sounds of Australia

children.content: A variety of iconic Australian recordings are added to the Sounds of Australia collection, as part of an annual update by the National Film and Sound Archive.

gallery.altText: Screengrab of a grey-haired man being wrestled into a car by a few policeman

thumbnail.altText: Screengrab of a grey-haired man being wrestled into a car by a few policeman

items.title: 'Wholly and solely systemic racism': Anger over closure of Indigenous health hub

children.content: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

image.altText: A woman with glasses in front of an Indigenous artwork.

items.title: NSW South Coast pub shooting leaves worker with 'life-changing injuries'

children.content: Police have arrested and charged a 41-year-old man over the shooting and alleged armed robbery at a pub in Narooma, on the NSW far south coast.

image.altText: Narooma Hotel

items.title: Australia aids Artemis II as NASA astronauts return to the Moon

children.content: Canberra-based communications systems will support NASA's first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century, helping astronauts orbit the Moon and return data.

gallery.altText: Three people look at space communications technology.
Astronauts wearing orange suits inside a spacecraft.

thumbnail.altText: Three people look at space communications technology.
Astronauts wearing orange suits inside a spacecraft.

items.title: ACT police, prosecutors accused of 'egregious conduct' over botched prosecution

children.content: Lawyers for a Canberra man cleared of 14 family violence charges are calling for an apology and compensation, after police found evidence the complainant fabricated some claims.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

image.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.

items.title: Man accused of failed Invasion Day rally bomb may enter insanity plea

children.content: A man accused of tossing a homemade "fragment bomb" filled with ball bearings and screws into a crowd in Perth's CBD flags through his lawyer that he may argue he is not criminally responsible for his actions due to insanity.

image.altText: Police speak to a man wearing a black butterfly print T-shirt, baseball cap and face mask

items.title: Socceroos smash Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell

children.content: The Socceroos have racked up quick second half goals to beat Curaçao 5-1 in their World Cup farewell match in Melbourne.

image.altText: Irankunda backflip

items.title: Australia has not yet reached 'tipping point' for WFH directive, says expert

children.content: The fuel crisis is putting pressure on workers who rely on car travel and those who meet with vulnerable clients, with fresh calls for frontline support staff to be exempt from any future fuel rationing mandates.

articles.title: Early government modelling shows how long fuel stockpile lasts if imports slow

children.content: Diesel stocks would last about six months if Australia's imports dropped by 20 per cent, according to modelling compiled for the federal government early in the Middle East war.

image.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

articles.title: What the halving of the fuel excise means for the price of your petrol

children.content: Australians should save about 26c a litre at the pump after the federal government announces a cut to the fuel excise.

image.altText: A petrol price sign with figures illuminated in red LED, surrounded by blue lights and next to a service station.

articles.title: Petrol stations tried to 'shape' the market — now it's shaping them

children.content: Petrol stations in our biggest cities hiked prices ahead of global oil prices, but experts say their profit margins are now much slimmer.

image.altText: Graphic of complex chart lines overlaid ontop of large blue droplet on top of faded oily texture and pale yellow background.

gallery.altText: A  woman sits in the driver seat of her car.

thumbnail.altText: A  woman sits in the driver seat of her car.

items.title: Thousands of Chinese social media users change profile photos to Kris Jenner

children.content: Thousands of Chinese social media users have started using images of US celebrity Kris Jenner as their profile photos in hopes it will bring them success and good fortune.

image.altText: A woman looks at the camera smiling.

items.title: Why the 'ultimate' Barbie fan event left visitors wanting a refund

children.content: Barbie fans who paid up to $656 for an "unforgettable experience" were met with a concrete-floored warehouse and flimsy pink cardboard cut-outs.

image.altText: A Barbie festival with a cheap cardboard cut out dream house, fake grass and a VW van

items.title: Prized WA tourism hotspots forced to close to holiday-makers for Easter

children.content: Famed holiday hotspots in Western Australia's north, including Exmouth and Coral Bay, are reluctantly turning away tourists ahead of the Easter holiday peak as they rebuild in the wake of Cyclone Narelle.

articles.title: Early government modelling shows how long fuel stockpile lasts if imports slow

children.content: Diesel stocks would last about six months if Australia's imports dropped by 20 per cent, according to modelling compiled for the federal government early in the Middle East war.

items.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adults

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has issued its decision to abolish junior pay rates for young adult employees in the retail, fast food, and pharmacy sectors while maintaining them for minors.

image.altText: A person holds a turquoise coffee mug underneath the spout of an espresso machine

items.title: ABC proposes new pay offer to staff after 24-hour strike

children.content: The updated ABC offer includes new provisions to ensure staff progress through pay bandings.

image.altText: People strike and hold up signs.

items.title: Tim Rogers is an Aussie rock legend — that doesn't impress the bank

children.content: The frontman of ARIA Hall Of Famers You Am I on work, family, and why Hourly, Daily is still special to him.

items.title: 'Why isn't it happening for me?' Three women on being perpetually single

children.content: Three women share their experiences of never having a partner — not by choice — and what that's like when you wish you did.

image.altText: Anna-Nicole Del Re smiling in a white dress

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

image.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.

items.title: Boaties find themselves in stand-off with stowaway sugar glider

children.content: The nocturnal marsupial, which typically spends the day sleeping in tree hollows, was discovered when the sails of the boat were opened.

A sugar glider wrapped in a blanket

items.title: Report card issued on Antarctic division's cultural reforms

children.content: Workplace reforms implemented by the Australian Antarctic Division (AAD) after two damning reports into the organisation's culture have led to some improvements but a review has found problems persist.

image.altText: Casey Station photographed from above.

items.title: Jackie O claiming 'at least' $82m in legal action against ARN Media

children.content: Former Sydney radio broadcaster Jackie "O" Henderson has filed legal action against employer ARN Media, following the collapse of the hit Kyle and Jackie O Show.

image.altText: A selfie of Sydney radio hosts Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O.

items.title: IOC's proposed gender test not fit for purpose, says man who discovered it

children.content: The scientist who discovered the SRY gene test that the International Olympic Committee is now relying on to determine athletes' eligibility for women's events says it should not be used in such a fashion.

image.altText: Olympic rings on a rainbow background.

items.title: Melbourne couple allegedly beat and starved woman enslaved in their home

children.content: A court has heard an Indonesian woman was allegedly threatened and coerced to provide domestic services while living with a Melbourne couple.

image.altText: A courtroom sketch shows a man scowling and a woman with a blank expression.

items.title: Hundreds gather to farewell 'Welcome to Country' pioneer

children.content: Rhoda Roberts AO is being remembered for her ability to laugh and embrace life, as well as her contribution to the Australian arts and Indigenous cultural movements.

thumbnail.altText: A woman stands in a theatre

items.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds

children.content: Nicotine-based vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer, according to a new review of evidence. Experts say there needs to be tougher enforcement of vape laws in Australia.

image.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

items.title: 'I'm sick of it': One in two Australians priced out of healthcare system

children.content: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

image.altText: A mum, a dad and their three children smile with forest behind them

items.title: The 10 pivotal events that led to the downfall of Essendon and Carlton

children.content: Essendon and Carlton were once mainstays when it came to the business end of the AFL season. Over the last quarter century, both clubs have fallen on hard times. Here's why.

items.title: NASA begins countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years

children.content: Four astronauts on the Artemis II rocket are set to blast off early Thursday morning.

image.altText: Four astronauts wearing blue jumpsuits standing in front of the tall Artemis II spacecraft on a clear day.

items.title: Iran's 'safe passage' signals a new global divide

children.content: China is now the world's largest crude oil importer, and Iran — though a discounted and often opaque supplier — remains a consistent part of that supply mix.

image.altText: China President Xi Jinping.

children.content: A shift towards modern technology is making a career in agriculture more appealing to a new generation of farmers.

items.title: Supermarket chicken could get cheaper due to grain breakthrough

children.content: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive livestock feed with systems that could lead to cheaper meat and eggs.

image.altText: UQ Professor Ian Godwin stands in a field of sorghum.

items.title: Cobram Estate praises America's 'grower-first mentality' after $260m deal

children.content: Australia's largest olive oil producer has completed its purchase of a massive olive grove in California, giving it about 70 per cent of the state's production.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and reaches up to some olives on a tree in an olive grove.

items.title: Five social media companies investigated over response to teen ban

children.content: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.
